figures.caption: Fig. 4: The visualization of grasp view. And scale-adaptive anchor can be determined on the minimal bounding sphere.
Fig. 5: The visualization of our datasets, demonstrating stable bimanual grasping of objects with varying geometries and sizes.
TABLE II: Performance comparison of dual-arm grasping on DexGraspNet and Objaverse datasets.

Fig. 2: The data synthesis pipeline for bimanual grasping. The GWS-based region selection and region-based grasp initialization aims to obtain grasp candidates with the potential to satisfy physical feasibility. And the decoupled force-closure-based optimization is to refine these initial candidates into physically valid bimanual grasps.

Fig. 3: BiDexGrasp Framework. Given the input object point cloud and pre-defined grasp view, the framework first predicts a pair of coordinated grasp views for the two hands by bimanual coordination module. Based on this view, we extract geometryadaptive object feature and determine the scale-adaptive grasp. Then the relative dexterous grasp diffusion is employed to predict relative dexterous grasp form the grasp anchor.
